Lucknow, Patna, Bhopal, Jaipur, ?
questions.solution:
All the places given in the question are capitals of states. 
 
So, the asked place would be Shimala beacuse it's a capital of Himachal Pradesh.

Hence, option C is correct.

questions.q: Four of the following five are alike in a certain way and so form a group. Which one of the following is different from the group?
questions.solution:
After a close look you will get that except 360 each number is one more than square of a natural number, ie
$\begin{aligned}
& 226=15^2+1 ; \quad 290=17^2+1 ; 170=13^2+1 ; \\
& 122=11^2+1
\end{aligned}$

questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Four of the following are similar in some respect. Find the odd one?
questions.solution:
The given numbers can be written as follows:
$143=12^2-1 ; 63=8^2-1 ; 195=14^2-1 ; 15=4^2-1$

But $257=16^2+1$
Obviously, except 257 others can be written in the form $x^2-1$.

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In this type questions, usually four groups of words are given. Three of them are similar to each other in some manner while one is different and this is to be chosen by the candidate as the answer.
Select the one which is different from the other three responses.
questions.solution:
Except Parallel, all others are types of angle.

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In this type of questions, certain pairs/groups of numbers are given out of which all except one are similar in some manner while one is different. The numbers in these similar pairs/groups may have the same property or may be related to each other according to the same rule. The candidate is required to choose the odd pair/group.
Which Pair does NOT belong with the others?
questions.solution:
In all other pairs, sum of the two numbers is 8.

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In this type of questions, certain pairs/groups of numbers are given out of which all except one are similar in some manner while one is different. The numbers in these similar pairs/groups may have the same property or may be related to each other according to the same rule. The candidate is required to choose the odd pair/group.
Which Pair does NOT belong with the others?
questions.solution:
All other pairs contain square and cube of the same number. e.g. 36 = 62 and 216 = 63.

Hence, option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In this type questions, usually four groups of words are given. Three of them are similar to each other in some manner while one is different and this is to be chosen by the candidate as the answer.
Four of the following five are alike in a certain way and so form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?
questions.solution:
Except Sugar, all others are liquid.

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In this type of questions, certain pairs/groups of numbers are given out of which all except one are similar in some manner while one is different. The numbers in these similar pairs/groups may have the same property or may be related to each other according to the same rule. The candidate is required to choose the odd pair/group.
Which Pair does NOT belong with the others?
questions.solution:
In all other pairs, 2nd number = (1st number × 3) + 5.

Hence, option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In this type questions, usually four groups of words are given. Three of them are similar to each other in some manner while one is different and this is to be chosen by the candidate as the answer.
Find the odd one out from the given alternatives:
questions.solution:
Except LAN, all others are memory types of a computer.

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In this type questions, usually four groups of words are given. Three of them are similar to each other in some manner while one is different and this is to be chosen by the candidate as the answer.
Find the odd one out from the given alternatives:
questions.solution:
Except Potato, all others are rich sources of Vitamin C.

Hence, option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In this type of questions, certain numbers are given, out of which all except one share some common property and hence are alike, while one is different and this number is to be chosen as the answer.
Choose the number which is DIFFERENT from the rest?
questions.solution:
All other numbers are cubes of odd numbers whereas 64 is a cube of an even number.

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In this type questions, usually four groups of words are given. Three of them are similar to each other in some manner while one is different and this is to be chosen by the candidate as the answer.
Find the odd one out from the given alternatives:
questions.solution:
Except Blurred all others are synonyms of ‘mistake’.

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In this type of questions, certain numbers are given, out of which all except one share some common property and hence are alike, while one is different and this number is to be chosen as the answer.
Choose the number which is DIFFERENT from the rest?
questions.solution:
Each of the numbers except 63, is a prime number.

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
